title: Jenny Dead
content:
Like a flower in the frost
        Sweet Jenny lies,
    With her frail hands calmly crossed,
        And close-shut eyes.

    Bring a candle, for the room
        Is dark and cold,
    Antechamber of the tomb - 
        O grief untold!

    Like a snowdrift is her bed,
        Dinted the snow,
    Faint frozen lines from foot to head, - 
        She lies below.

    Turn from off her shrouded face
        The frigid sheet....
    Death hath doubled all her grace - 
        O Jenny, sweet!
meaning:
"Jenny Dead" is a quintessential example of Richard Le Gallienne's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
